content.text:
The error is an ESLint failure in `web/app/projects/page.tsx:95` — calling `setState` synchronously within a `useEffect`. The `measure()` function is called directly in the effect body, which triggers a state update. The fix is to only call `measure()` inside the `ResizeObserver` callback (which is async), not synchronously at the top of the effect.

Let me read the relevant code.

The issue is line 95: `measure()` is called synchronously in the effect, which sets state via `setVisibleCount`. The lint rule says setState shouldn't be called synchronously in an effect body — only in callbacks from external system subscriptions (like the `ResizeObserver` callback on line 96, which is fine).

The fix: use `requestAnimationFrame` to defer the initial measurement, making it asynchronous.

content.content: The file /Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/web/app/projects/page.tsx has been updated successfully.

lines: const hasOverflow = visibleCount !== null && visibleCount < items.length;

content.text: The error is fixed — 0 errors now (the 2 remaining warnings are pre-existing and unrelated). The fix wraps the initial `measure()` call in `requestAnimationFrame` so setState isn't called synchronously in the effect body, which satisfies the `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` rule. The cleanup also properly cancels the animation frame.

content.text: Pushed. The commit `9e8a872` fixes the ESLint `react-hooks/set-state-in-effect` error by deferring the initial `measure()` call via `requestAnimationFrame`.
